title: Humans are made to handle raw animal foods

chunks.title: Introduction to Raw Egg Consumption
chunks.summary: The speakers share personal experiences regarding their consumption of raw eggs, with one recounting an instance where they got sick after eating old eggs. They discuss their conviction about the risks of salmonella and how they believe regular consumption has built their resilience.
chunks.transcript: okay two questions you ever get sick from raw eggs never okay me neither actually that's not true i got sick from eating raw eggs once and it was when i was living in seattle and i found a dozen eggs in a friend's refrigerator and i had no idea how long they've been there maybe six months eight months and i was like in residency i thought i'm gonna eat these so i ate the raw egg eating salmonella or equal or whatever it is you know like i'm convinced that it's on there we're just inoculated i mean because i'm sure you've also had a handful a day probably for years and at same we just break them and we eat them so i'm convinced we're getting exposed to it every day we've just slowly gotten exposed to it and we can handle it now i think it's a great point you know every once chunks.title: Body Resilience and Exposure to Bacteria
chunks.summary: They explore the idea that the human body is more resilient to bacteria like salmonella and E. coli than commonly perceived. Anecdotes about nausea after eating meat highlight how the body can sometimes react to pathogens but usually adapts over time.
chunks.transcript: oh yeah oh yeah and i always said you know um every once in a while i get people that are like hey aren't you worried about salmonella i'm like liver king worried about someone else salmonella should be worried about liver king you know people worry about things in a way that they make themselves sick right and if it's something that exists in nature if it's something that we've been doing for hundreds of thousands even millions of years we've designed a way to deal with it and some people can say oh well that way is fire but to have the ability to create fire at will hasn't always been the case you know and even today modern day primitive culture tribes still always incorporate some raw foods right so we always try and do the same as well and so i'm not convinced that you got to do everything cooked or everything raw but i do think you should do both and so i we figured this out when i say we figured it out it's not like in our lifetime we figured it out right the the human the species has figured this out so yeah i think it's a whole lot to do

chunks.title: Cultural and Historical Perspectives on Raw Food
chunks.summary: The speakers argue that human beings have managed to consume raw foods for thousands of years and suggest that modern concerns about food safety are largely based on social norms. They also emphasize the importance of including both raw and cooked foods in the diet.
chunks.transcript: with nothing when people are worried about aren't you gonna get sick it's just a social norm that this what we've been fed and people are getting themselves sick worrying about it i think that there's this whole hygiene hypothesis argument on the backside saying maybe we're too clean you know like maybe we should let our kids play in the dirt maybe we should let our kids you know fall down and skin their knee maybe we should let our kids eat some raw meat or eat some undercooked meat or you know get sick every once in a while maybe we're all

chunks.title: Hygiene Hypothesis and Childhood Exposure
chunks.summary: Discussing the hygiene hypothesis, they suggest that children should be allowed to engage in messy play and consume raw or undercooked food occasionally. They propose that exposure to certain pathogens can enhance resilience and that minor illnesses might be a natural part of development.
